answers.answer: B) Use a multi-stage build: install dependencies and build in a full node image, then copy only the production artifacts into a node:alpine runtime image. Multi-stage builds dramatically reduce image size by excluding build tools and dev dependencies. Alpine runtime is ~50MB vs ~1GB for full node. Option A is bad practice (unpinned versions). Option C saves minimal space. Option D reduces layers but minimal impact.

GIT WORKFLOW:

1. Squash: git rebase -i HEAD~8, change commits 4 and 6 to 'fixup'
2. Rebase: git fetch origin main; git rebase origin/main
3. Resolve conflict: Edit src/auth/middleware.ts to keep BOTH main's imports AND your auth logic. git add; git rebase --continue
4. Cherry-pick: git cherry-pick abc1234
5. Push: git push --force-with-lease origin feature/auth-v2; gh pr create --base main --head feature/auth-v2 --title 'feat: Auth v2 (replaces #123)'

Recovery: git rebase --abort, git cherry-pick --abort, git reflog
